message:
fix(service): correct readiness tracking in Filter/Reconnect, add RetryError wrapper

Filter:
- poll_ready now short-circuits if readiness was already observed,
  preventing redundant inner polls that could consume connection-pool
  slots or advance internal state machines
- call() only consumes ready_observed when the predicate accepts the
  request; rejected requests preserve the readiness window since the
  inner service may hold reserved state
- Add StrictReservingService test that verifies the reservation
  semantics with a capacity-limited inner service

Reconnect:
- Add ready_observed field enforcing the poll_ready→call contract:
  each successful poll_ready authorizes exactly one call, and calling
  without prior readiness returns NotReady
- Add RefreshPendingGuard for epoch-aware reconnection that prevents
  stale refresh_pending flags when the service epoch advances during
  a reconnection attempt
- Reset ready_observed on reconnect and invalidate_inner

Retry:
- Introduce RetryError<E> enum wrapping Inner(E) and
  PolledAfterCompletion, replacing the bare S::Error propagation
- This makes retry-level errors distinguishable from inner service
  errors at the type level
- Re-export RetryError from service::mod

service_verification test updated to match on RetryError::Inner.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
